title: Silent failure in DaoManager::build_dao with unwrap_or(0)
description:
File: rust/lichen-rpl/src/routing.rs
Lines: 314, 321-322, 331

The build_dao() method uses unwrap_or(0) for write operations:
```rust
let mut pos = dao.write_to(&mut buf).unwrap_or(0);
...
let n = target.write_to(&mut tmp).unwrap_or(0);
...
pos += transit.write_to(&mut buf[pos..]).unwrap_or(0);
```

Impact: If any write fails (which shouldn't happen with correctly sized buffers, but could during future refactoring), the code silently continues:
1. dao.write_to failing returns pos=0, meaning options overwrite the DAO base
2. target.write_to failing means no target bytes are copied
3. transit.write_to failing means no transit info is added

All these would produce malformed DAOs that would be rejected by receivers or cause routing failures.

While the current buffer sizes are correct (64 bytes > 62 needed), this pattern is fragile and hides errors.

Fix: Propagate errors or use expect() with clear message:
```rust
let mut pos = dao.write_to(&mut buf).expect("DAO base fits in 64-byte buffer");
```
Or return Result<Vec<u8>, RplError> from build_dao().
close_reason: Already fixed: code now uses .expect() with clear messages instead of unwrap_or(0)
